arts.ttl: Barcelona come under fire as official statement accuses club of breaking FIFA rules
arts.sum: Barcelona's rivals Osasuna have issued a statement explaining their position after accusing the Catalan club of breaking FIFA rules with their team selection in Thursday's meeting

arts.ttl: Dani Olmo injury: The games Barcelona playmaker is set to miss
arts.sum: Dani Olmo is one of Barcelona's most valuable assets but looks set for an unforeseen spell on the sidelines at just the wrong time.

arts.ttl: Barcelona confirm Dani Olmo injury: A look at which matches the eight-goal attacker will miss
arts.sum: Barcelona attacker Dani Olmo will be sidelined for approximately three weeks with the right adductor injury that he suffered against Osasuna.

arts.ttl: Ex-Barca defender Alves has rape conviction quashed
arts.sum: Former Barcelona and Brazil defender Dani Alves has a sexual assault conviction overturned on appeal by a Spanish court.

arts.ttl: Osasuna launch appeal over Inigo Martinez 'ineligibility' and Barcelona have questions to answer amid fears of points deduction
arts.sum: Osasuna launch an official appeal to the Spanish Football Federation, claiming that Inigo Martinez was not eligible to take to the field for Barcelona on Thursday.

arts.ttl: Wojciech Szczesny hits impressive Barcelona landmark
arts.sum: Wojciech Szczesny’s incredible season at Barcelona is set to break more new ground this weekend at home to rivals Girona. The former Poland international battled through a tough start to life to become the first choice goalkeeper at Barcelona, and he has started to deliver with his performances in 2025. Following a season-ending injury to […] The post Wojciech Szczesny hits impressive Barcelona landmark appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Barcelona: Osasuna launch appeal claiming Inigo Martinez was ineligible for LaLiga clash
arts.sum: Osasuna adamant defender should not have played in rescheduled fixture after withdrawing from Spain duty through injury

arts.ttl: Torre still happy at Barca - ‘amazing to be here with these monsters’
arts.sum: Pablo Torre may not be enjoying many minutes at Barcelona but says he’s still loving life at the club. The midfielder came on at half-time of Thursday’s win over Osasuna and put in another impressive cameo, playing a role in Barca’s third goal.

arts.ttl: Barcelona in hot water over illegal lineup
arts.sum: “If a player does not attend or withdraws from the national team due to injury, he may not play for his club for five days after the last match of the international window unless the national federation expressly releases him,” states the FIFA regulation Osasuna is citing in its appeal. As reported by AS and officially confirmed by the club, Osasuna believes that Barcelona fielded an ineligible player due to the presence of Iñigo Martínez on the pitch.

arts.ttl: Barcelona at risk of points deduction after Osasuna launch official appeal
arts.sum: Osasuna have filed a formal complaint against Barcelona's La Liga victory.
